expectations.text: Iterates using the write → recalculate → read pattern multiple times to converge on the answer
expectations.evidence: The report uses an analytical formula to solve directly: 'Rev_Y1 = (Target_EBITDA_Y3 + $2,205,000) / (1.21 * 0.42)'. No iteration or write-recalculate-read pattern was used.

expectations.text: Writes Inp_Rev_Y1 values via idfa-ops write command (not manual cell edits)
expectations.evidence: No mention of idfa-ops write command. The solution is computed analytically.

expectations.text: Reads EBITDA_Y3 via idfa-ops read command after each recalculation
expectations.evidence: No idfa-ops read command used. EBITDA_Y3 was computed analytically.

expectations.text: Converges to a solution within 1% tolerance of the $3,500,000 target
expectations.evidence: The analytical solution produces exactly $3,500,000 EBITDA_Y3 (shown in verification table), which is within 1% tolerance by definition.

expectations.text: Leaves the final solved value written in the model (does NOT revert to the original 10M)
expectations.evidence: The report shows the analytical result but there is no evidence the value was written to the model. It says the xlsx exists but doesn't confirm the value was persisted.

expectations.text: Reports the required Inp_Rev_Y1 value and the resulting EBITDA_Y3, both read from the model
expectations.evidence: Reports required Inp_Rev_Y1 = $11,225,895.32 and resulting EBITDA_Y3 = $3,500,000, but these come from analytical calculation, not model reads.
